text:
To determine the combined weight of the two pennies, we start by finding the weight of the 2010 penny. According to the problem, the 2010 penny is three-quarters of the weight of the 1959 penny. Since the 1959 penny weighs 48 grains, we can calculate the weight of the 2010 penny as follows:

\[
\text{Weight of the 2010 penny} = \frac{3}{4} \times 48 \text{ grains} = 36 \text{ grains}
\]

Next, we add the weight of the 2010 penny to the weight of the 1959 penny to find the combined weight:

\[
\text{Combined weight} = 36 \text{ grains} + 48 \text{ grains} = 84 \text{ grains}
\]

Therefore, the combined weight of the two pennies is \(\boxed{84}\) grains.
